folio.label: 56v
folio.l: Thei can nat sen , in that , non auauntage
As in none other wey , saue in mariage
¢ Than saw they æäe Inne , swicú difficulte
By weye of resou~ , for to speke al pleyne
By cause that there was , swiche diuäsite
Bytwene here bothe lawes , that they seyne
They trowe that no cristene pénce , wolde feyne
Wedden his childe , vn to oure lawes swete
That vs was taugút , by Mahoun oure ¸phete
¢ And he answered , rather than I lese
Custaunce , I wol be cristened doutelesse
I mot ben hires , I may noon other chese
I prey ©ow , holde ©oure argumentez in pes
Sauetú my lyf , and betú nougút reccheles
To getyn hire , that hatú my lyf in cure
For in this woo , I may not longe endure

note.notetxt: 133 The ink after this section in the page seem a bit darker, but still same shade of lighter brown. Notice compression of final lines.ODR

folio.l: ¢ What nedetú grettere , dilatacione
I seye be tretys , and embassatrye
And by the Popes mediacione
And alle the chirche , and al the chyualrie
That in destruccione , of maumentrie
And in encresse , of cristes lawe dere
They ben accorded , so as ©e shuln here
¢ How æ the Soudan , and his baronage
And alle hise lieges , shulde I cristened be
And he shal haue Custaunce , in mariage
And certeyne gold , I not what quantite
And her to founden , sufficeant seurte
This same accord , was sworn~ on either syde
Now fair Custaunce , almygúty god the gyde
¢ Now wolde su~me men , wayten as I gesse
That I shulde tellen , al the purueance
That the Em¹our , of his grete noblesse
Hatú shapen for his dougúter , Dame Custaunce
Wel may men knowen , that so grete ordinaunce
May noman telle , in a litel clause
As was arrayed ,. for so heygú a cause
¢ Bisshopes ben shapen , witú hire for to wende
Lordes ladies , knygútes of renoune
And othere folk I nowe , this is the ende
And notified is , thurgú ou©t the toun~
That euäy wygút , witú gret deuociouö
Shulde preyen crist , that he this mariage
Receyue in gree , and spede this viage
